steps.title: Identify slope and y-intercept
steps.content: Slope-intercept form is $y = mx + b$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the $y$-intercept. The slope is given as $m = 6$. The point $(0, 4)$ has $x = 0$, which means it is the $y$-intercept, so $b = 4$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $y = -4x + 6$: Slope is $-4$, not $6$. Eliminated.
  • B) $y = -4x - 6$: Slope is $-4$ and $y$-intercept is $-6$. Neither matches. Eliminated.
  • C) $y = 6x + 4$: Slope $6$ and $y$-intercept $4$. Matches.
  • D) $y = 6x - 4$: Slope $6$ is right, but $y$-intercept is $-4$, not $4$. Eliminated.
That confirms C is correct.
